\noindent We saw in Section 3.2.3 that the finiteness of
the population from which we sample has a
noticeable effect when the sample size is greater
than 10\% of the population size. In the example
of the mean monthly payroll ($n = 50$, $N =
2,222$), it can be expected that this is the case
if we want the precision around the total monthly
payroll to be less than 300,000.
The confidence interval incorporating the finite-population
correction factor is given by Equation 3.10:

\par\addvspace{\topsep}
\begingroup
\fvset{listparameters={%
  \setlength{\topsep}{0pt}%
  \setlength{\partopsep}{0pt}%
  \setlength{\parsep}{0pt}%
  \setlength{\itemsep}{0pt}%
}}
\begin{Verbatim}[breaklines=true,formatcom=\color{ada_blue}]
E = 300000
tval = t.ppf(0.975, df=(n - 1))
gamma = E**2 / (N * tval**2 * np.std(sample1["gross"])**2)
(N / (1 + gamma))
\end{Verbatim}
\begin{Verbatim}[breaklines=true,formatcom=\color{ada_light_blue}]
[1] 4.242586

[1] 423.8366
\end{Verbatim}
\endgroup
\par\addvspace{\topsep}
